data.title: Data Breach Affects Over 50,000 Patients at Utah Medical Group
data.description: Rocky Mountain Associated Physicians, a medical group based in Utah, has reported a data breach impacting 50,640 individuals. According to NS3.AI, the breach involved unauthorized access to systems containing sensitive patient information. The compromised data may include personal, medical, insurance, and financial details of some patients.

data.title: Iran to Implement Fees for Security and Environmental Services in Strait of Hormuz
data.description: Iran's Supreme National Security Council has announced plans to impose fees related to security, safety, and environmental protection services for vessels passing through the Strait of Hormuz. According to NS3.AI, this strategic move aims to enhance control over the vital maritime passage, which is crucial for global oil transportation. The council emphasized the importance of maintaining safety and environmental standards in the region, which sees a significant portion of the world's oil shipments. The decision reflects Iran's ongoing efforts to assert its influence in the area and ensure compliance with international maritime regulations.

data.title: Samuel Alito's Ongoing Influence in U.S. Legal and Cultural Battles
data.description: A new biography highlights U.S. Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito's enduring role in cultural and legal debates that trace back to the 1960s. Bloomberg posted on X, emphasizing Alito's significant influence as a legal advocate for U.S. President Donald Trump. The biography suggests that Alito's judicial philosophy and decisions continue to shape pivotal cultural and legal issues in the United States. His involvement in these matters underscores his lasting impact on the nation's legal landscape.

data.title: Company Faces Challenges with Inaccessible Bitcoin Wallet
data.description:
A company has revealed difficulties in accessing a Bitcoin wallet containing 4,500 BTC due to missing private keys. According to CoinDesk, the private keys are linked to a former CEO who is currently unaccounted for, complicating efforts to retrieve the funds. The situation underscores the importance of secure key management in the cryptocurrency industry, where the loss of access to private keys can result in significant financial setbacks.

The company is exploring potential solutions to regain access to the wallet, but the absence of the former CEO poses a significant hurdle. This incident highlights the risks associated with digital asset management and the critical need for robust security protocols. As the cryptocurrency market continues to evolve, companies are increasingly focused on safeguarding their assets against such vulnerabilities. The case serves as a reminder of the challenges faced by businesses in maintaining control over their digital holdings.

data.title: Trump Signs Executive Order to Boost Psychedelic Research for PTSD Treatment
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has signed an executive order aimed at accelerating research and access to psychedelics for treating post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD). Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of this move in exploring alternative treatments for PTSD, particularly those used outside the United States. The executive order seeks to facilitate scientific studies and potentially integrate these substances into therapeutic practices, reflecting a growing interest in the potential benefits of psychedelics in mental health care.

data.title: Iran's Supreme National Security Agency: Iran's control over the Strait of Hormuz includes collecting fees related to security, safety, and environmental protection services.
data.description: Iran's Supreme National Security Agency stated that Iran's control over the Strait of Hormuz includes collecting fees related to security, safety, and environmental protection services. (Iranian state media)

data.title: US President Trump: We are having very good talks with Iran, and Iran cannot blackmail us.
data.description: US President Trump: We are having very good talks (with Iran). We are talking to them. They want to close the Strait of Hormuz again, and Iran cannot blackmail us. There will be some information before the end of today. (Jinshi)

data.title: Blockstream CEO: Bitcoin price may soon approach $500,000 to $1 million
data.description: Blockstream CEO Adam Back pointed out that Bitcoin's role as a store of value and growing institutional demand could push its price to $500,000 to $1 million ahead of expectations. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Islamabad upgrades traffic control in preparation for US-Iran negotiations
data.description: Early this morning (April 18), a ban on heavy vehicles entering Islamabad officially took effect. Control measures at local long-distance bus stations were tightened, and subway and bus services were shortened. The core logic behind Islamabad's transportation upgrades lies in enhancing the atmosphere of mutual trust, using absolute physical security to compensate for weak foundations in external trust, and proving to Washington and Tehran that the Islamabad negotiating table is not only a geographical transit point, but also an absolute safe haven in terms of information and security. Currently, the Pakistani government has not yet announced a timetable for the new round of US-Iran negotiations. (CCTV International News)

data.title: Iran's Supreme National Security Agency: Iran is determined to control traffic in the Strait of Hormuz until the war is completely over.
data.description: Iran's Supreme National Security Agency stated that Iran is determined to control traffic in the Strait of Hormuz until the war is completely over and lasting peace is achieved in the region. (Iranian state media)

data.title: Islamabad Implements Heavy Vehicle Ban to Enhance Security
data.description: Today, April 18, Islamabad, the capital of Pakistan, has officially enforced a ban on heavy vehicles entering the city. According to Odaily, local long-distance bus stations have tightened controls, and metro and bus services have reduced their operating hours. The core logic behind this transportation upgrade in Islamabad is to enhance an atmosphere of 'mutual trust' by ensuring absolute physical security, compensating for the weak foundation of diplomatic trust. This move aims to demonstrate to Washington and Tehran that Islamabad is not only a geographical transit point but also an island of absolute security in terms of information and safety. Currently, the Pakistani government has not announced a timetable for the new round of U.S.-Iran negotiations.

data.title: Iran's Conditional Stance on Potential War Escalation
data.description: Iran has indicated that it would fully commit to military action if it reenters the conflict, according to NS3.AI. The statement from Iranian officials emphasized that any escalation would be contingent upon specific circumstances, rather than being an immediate decision. This conditional approach highlights Iran's strategic considerations in the ongoing geopolitical tensions.

data.title: An institutional source responded to DeepSeek's first external funding round: The news is very likely true, but we are currently unable to invest at all.
data.description: On April 18th, news that DeepSeek, a leading Chinese AI company, might be launching its first external financing round continued to circulate within the industry. A recent report from a tech and business-focused media outlet stated that DeepSeek is in talks to introduce external capital for the first time, hoping to bolster its cash reserves and gain a more advantageous position in the high-cost competition of developing top-tier AI models. Previously, the company had repeatedly rejected investment offers from leading Chinese venture capital firms and tech giants. However, according to two sources, the startup has recently begun contacting investors, planning to raise at least $300 million at a valuation of at least $10 billion. In response, an individual from a large state-owned equity institution stated, "We have received feedback that the news of DeepSeek launching its first external financing round is very likely true; we are currently unable to invest at all," adding, "More information is not convenient to disclose." Furthermore, from noon to afternoon on April 18th, attempts to contact two senior executives at Huanfang Quantitative Research via personal phone and WeChat messages were made to verify the DeepSeek financing news, but as of press time, neither executive answered their calls, and their WeChat messages went unanswered. (China Securities Journal)

data.title: An address suspected of being the RAVE operator holds 750 million tokens, worth over $10.3 billion.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, a group of addresses suspected of being used by RAVE market manipulators currently holds 750 million tokens, with a total value of over $10.3 billion, representing a 75% concentration of token holdings.

data.title: Iranian media: Iran says it will go all out if it returns to war.
data.description: According to Iranian media Fars News, Iran has stated that it will go all out if it returns to war. Previously, Iran's First Vice President stated that Iran would take responsibility for the management of the Strait of Hormuz. The message is clear: either the United States grants us rights at the negotiating table, or we go to war. (Jinshi)

data.title: Polymarket Predicts Low Probability of Strait of Hormuz Normalization by April
data.description:
The probability of the Strait of Hormuz returning to normal operations by April 30 has significantly dropped to 30%, marking a 24% decrease over the past 24 hours. According to Odaily, the trading volume for this event contract has exceeded $15.86 million.

The contract stipulates that if the International Monetary Fund's Portwatch reports a seven-day moving average of 60 or more ships arriving at the Strait of Hormuz on any date from the market's creation until May 31, 2026, the market will resolve as 'Yes.' Otherwise, it will resolve as 'No.' The daily count includes container ships, bulk carriers, roll-on/roll-off ships, general cargo ships, and tankers, excluding those not reported by the IMF Portwatch.

The decline in probability is attributed to the U.S. imposing a maritime blockade on Iran and making excessive demands in negotiations. Iran has not agreed to a new round of talks with the U.S., emphasizing that avoiding excessive demands is crucial for continuing negotiations. Iran has stated it is unwilling to engage in prolonged and meaningless discussions. Several ships attempting to pass through the Strait of Hormuz have turned back. The maritime intelligence company "Tanker Trackers" reported on social media that two Indian ships trying to cross the strait were forced to retreat by the Iranian Revolutionary Guard Navy, with gunfire occurring during the incident.

data.title: Iran Signals Readiness for Full Commitment if Conflict Resumes
data.description: Iran has declared its readiness to fully commit if it returns to conflict, according to Odaily. This statement follows remarks from Iran's First Vice President, who emphasized Iran's responsibility for managing the Strait of Hormuz. The Vice President stated that the United States must either grant rights at the negotiation table or face confrontation on the battlefield.

data.title: Concerns Over RAVE Token Concentration as Major Holder Emerges
data.description: An analyst, @ai_9684xtpa, posted on X about a suspected major holder of the RAVE token, who currently possesses 750 million tokens valued at over $103 billion. This concentration accounts for 75% of the total supply. Observers interested in tracking further on-chain movements of the token are advised to set up alerts by selecting RAVE and adjusting the alert amount accordingly.

data.title: Bitcoin Long-Term Holder Supply Increases Significantly
data.description: Bitcoin's long-term holder supply has seen a notable increase, rising from 5.26 million BTC in January to 8.32 million BTC by April 16. According to NS3.AI, the LTH Realized Supply metric, which monitors Bitcoin that has remained unmoved for over 155 days, highlights this growth. This trend indicates a growing confidence among long-term investors in the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: Ethereum Co-Founder Discusses Blockchain Evolution and Quantum Computing Concerns
data.description:
Ethereum co-founder recently shared insights on the platform's development, highlighting advancements through MetaMask, stablecoins, and tokenization. According to CoinDesk, he emphasized the importance of these elements in Ethereum's ongoing evolution, suggesting that they play a crucial role in enhancing the blockchain's functionality and accessibility.

During the discussion, the co-founder addressed the potential impact of quantum computing on blockchain technology. He downplayed the threat, describing it as a long-term issue that can be managed effectively. This perspective suggests confidence in the blockchain community's ability to adapt and innovate in response to emerging technological challenges. The conversation also touched on the broader implications of Ethereum's growth, including its influence on the digital economy and the potential for further integration into various sectors. The co-founder’s remarks underscore the dynamic nature of blockchain technology and its capacity to evolve in response to both opportunities and challenges.
